reasoning: Lilly King set the 100m long course breaststroke world record of 1:04.13 in 2017, and it has stood for 8 years. I verified that this record was not broken at the 2025 World Aquatics Championships in Singapore, where Anna Elendt won gold with a time of 1:05.19 (a full second slower). Since the rest of the year (late autumn and winter) primarily consists of short course (25m) competitions, there are very few opportunities for long course world records to be broken between October 2025 and January 2026. Therefore, the probability that the record will hold is exceedingly high, only decreasing slightly for the later resolution dates to reflect the marginally longer time horizon.
